concerns.text: Task T013 ('Implement code/ingest.py to download Dst and Kp indices') is semantically weakened. Spec FR-003 and Constitution Principle VI explicitly mandate downloading BOTH Dst and Kp indices. The task description groups them but the implementation detail in T011-T013 only explicitly lists Dst in the logic breakdown, risking the silent omission of Kp data retrieval which is a required input for the 'GeomagneticStorm' entity defined in the spec.

Tasks T022-T029 describe computing correlations and p-values but lack an explicit task to implement the multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg) mandated by FR-005 and US-2. Without a dedicated task to 'apply correction to p-values', the deliverable will likely fall short of the requirement to control family-wise error rate at α≤0.05, constituting a silent relaxation of the statistical rigor.

Task T034 ('Implement threshold identification... with justification citing NOAA SWPC definition document') is a semantic weakening of FR-007. The spec requires the system to 'explicitly justify' by citing the specific document in the output artifacts. The task only mentions 'justification citing' in the description, failing to mandate the implementation of a mechanism to inject this specific citation into the `results/metrics.json` or report, risking a deliverable that calculates the threshold but fails the provenance requirement.

Tasks T006-T008 cover versioning and directory setup but lack an explicit task to instrument the *entire* pipeline (Ingest, Align, Analyze) for end-to-end execution time and RAM measurement. FR-012 and SC-004 require measuring total execution time and peak RAM to verify constraints. T030 only covers US2. Without a foundational task to wrap the main entry point (`main.py`) with global profiling, the system cannot verify the global 6h/7GB constraint, violating the success criteria.
